business.type_label: healthy recipe blog + digital course/ebook seller

engine.ladder_reasoning: The recipes ARE the draw and the proof — visitors browse the content itself to decide to stay/subscribe/buy. Not a defined-expertise pitch (trust), not a physical place, not a single ask (quick-yes).

goal.one_action: no single action — site juggles email opt-in, external course checkout, ebook sales, and affiliate-shop clicks

structure.archetype_note: Content-hub blog with recipe category grids + multiple monetization funnels (course, ebooks, affiliate). Sprawling multi-page (21 visible), not a single conversion page.

mapped.observed_as: Instant Pot Recipes / Meal Prep Recipes / Salads
SIGN UP (31 Days of Healthy Dinners)
Next, join the community! + social links

unmapped.observed: Philosophy (Real-World Approach / Consistency Over Perfection / Green Ingredient Focus / Nutritional Enjoyment / Personalized Diet Philosophy)
unmapped.why_no_fit: A multi-part manifesto/values page — closest to founderNote/objectionHandling but it's really an editorial belief-system page with no direct conversion role

unmapped.observed: Shop (Kitchen Appliances / Storage Containers / Cookware / Utensils affiliate category grids)
unmapped.why_no_fit: An affiliate-link category hub (no prices/cart) — not a real storefront/catalog, not features/pricing; it's a monetized link directory

proposed_new.name: recipe-index / content-category-grid
proposed_new.evidence: Home + category pages are grids of recipe cards with 'VIEW MORE' paginated category links — a content-browsing archetype distinct from a product catalog or portfolio gallery

proposed_new.name: affiliate-hub
proposed_new.evidence: Shop page is affiliate-link category grid with no prices/cart — monetization via outbound links, not commerce the taxonomy models

confidence.why: Nav/sections/CTAs captured full, but meta_description, embeds, and price data are null/empty (commerce.prices_shown false) — ebook/course pricing under-captured. Also the site is out-of-ICP (multi-funnel content publisher), so single-engine fit is inherently approximate.

gap_findings: Site's job is NOT one conversion action — it runs 4 parallel funnels (email list, external ThriveCart course, ebook sales, Amazon affiliate shop). Taxonomy assumes one primary conversion; no way to express a content-hub/blog monetized by multiple downstream paths.
Unmapped: recipe-index / content-category-grid archetype (browsing editorial content) has no canonical section — mapped loosely to hero/useCases.
Unmapped: affiliate-hub 'Shop' (link directory, no cart/prices) fits neither catalog nor pricing cleanly.
Proposed capabilities recipe-index and affiliate-hub are not in the closed vocab.
